skill.name: agent-browser
skill.description: Browser automation for AI agents via inference.sh. Navigate web pages, interact with elements using @e refs, take screenshots, record video. Capabilities: web scraping, form filling, clicking, typing, drag-drop, file upload, JavaScript execution. Use for: web automation, data extraction, testing, agent browsing, research. Triggers: browser, web automation, scrape, navigate, click, fill form, screenshot, browse web, playwright, headless browser, web agent, surf internet, record video
skill.summary: Browser automation for AI agents via inference.sh. Navigate web pages, interact with elements using @e refs, take screenshots, record video.
skill.author: infsh-skills

security_audit.summary: Static analysis detected 606 potential security issues, primarily shell command patterns in documentation files. After evaluation, all findings are FALSE POSITIVES or informational. The skill is a legitimate browser automation tool that uses the belt CLI to control Playwright. Shell commands are documented usage examples, not execution vulnerabilities. Browser credential access and session state are expected behavior for this tool type.

medium_findings.title: Shell Command Usage in Documentation
medium_findings.description: 501 shell command patterns detected in documentation files (authentication.md, commands.md, proxy-support.md, etc.). These are example commands showing how to use the belt CLI tool, not actual code execution vulnerabilities.
medium_findings.confidence_reasoning: Documentation patterns flagged as shell commands are example usage for the belt CLI tool, not actual execution vulnerabilities. The skill delegates to a controlled CLI interface.

medium_findings.title: Browser Credential and Storage Access
medium_findings.description: Skill accesses browser session state including cookies, LocalStorage, and SessionStorage. This is expected behavior for browser automation tools and is documented for legitimate use cases.
medium_findings.confidence_reasoning: Accessing browser state (cookies, storage) is the intended function of a browser automation tool, not a security vulnerability.

medium_findings.title: Network Access for Browser Service
medium_findings.description: Skill communicates with inference.sh service for browser control. URLs in documentation are legitimate service endpoints.
medium_findings.confidence_reasoning: Network access is to the inference.sh service for browser automation, not data exfiltration. All URLs are legitimate service endpoints.

medium_findings.title: Path Traversal Patterns in Documentation
medium_findings.description: Path traversal patterns (../) found in documentation are relative paths for file references, not actual path traversal vulnerabilities.
medium_findings.confidence_reasoning: These are relative paths in markdown documentation for linking between files, not filesystem vulnerabilities.

content.user_title: Automate browser tasks with AI agents
content.value_statement: AI agents need to interact with web pages but lack browser capabilities. This skill provides headless browser automation through simple shell commands, enabling web scraping, form filling, and automated testing workflows.
actual_capabilities: Navigate to URLs and capture page state with interactive element references
Click, fill forms, drag-drop, and interact with web page elements using @e refs
Take screenshots and record videos of browser sessions
Execute JavaScript code directly in the browser page context
Handle authentication flows including OAuth and two-factor authentication
Manage multiple browser sessions in parallel for concurrent workflows
limitations: Requires inference.sh account and belt CLI installation
Cannot interact with CAPTCHA or complex anti-bot protection systems
Video recording must be enabled at session start to capture footage
Sessions do not persist across server restarts

use_cases.title: Web data extraction for research
use_cases.description: Extract structured data from websites by navigating pages, filling search forms, and collecting page content. Useful for market research, competitor analysis, and data collection tasks.

use_cases.title: Automated form submission workflows
use_cases.description: Automate repetitive form filling tasks like job applications, survey completion, or data entry. Handle multi-step forms with conditional navigation and verification.

use_cases.title: Browser-based API testing
use_cases.description: Test web applications that require browser context, such as SPAs with client-side authentication or JavaScript-heavy workflows. Capture screenshots and video for documentation.

prompt_templates.title: Open website and explore
prompt_templates.prompt: Use the agent-browser skill to open https://example.com, take a screenshot, and list all clickable elements on the page.

prompt_templates.title: Login and verify access
prompt_templates.prompt: Use agent-browser to log into https://app.example.com with email user@example.com and password from the PASSWORD environment variable. After login, verify you can access the dashboard page and take a screenshot.

prompt_templates.title: Multi-step form automation
prompt_templates.prompt: Create a script using agent-browser to fill a multi-step registration form: open the form, fill step 1 with name and email, proceed to step 2, fill address details, submit, and verify success message.

prompt_templates.title: Parallel session data collection
prompt_templates.prompt: Use agent-browser to open three different product pages in parallel sessions, extract prices and availability from each, and close all sessions. Compare the collected data.

output_examples.input: Open example.com and list clickable elements
output_examples.output:

Clickable Elements:

- @e2 [a] 'Domains' href='https://example.com/domains'
- @e3 [button] 'Get Started'
- @e4 [input type='email'] placeholder='Enter your email'

Screenshot saved to: ./screenshots/example.com_20240101.png

output_examples.output:

2. Filled email field with user@example.com
3. Filled password field

5. Waited for redirect

best_practices: Always use environment variables for sensitive data like passwords instead of hardcoding credentials in scripts
Re-snapshot the page after any navigation or DOM changes to get fresh element references before interacting
Enable video recording only during debugging to avoid capturing sensitive information in production
anti_patterns: Do not hardcode passwords or API keys directly in scripts or command arguments
Do not skip the snapshot step after page navigation - stale element references will cause failures
Do not leave sessions open unnecessarily - always close sessions when done to free resources

faq.question: What is inference.sh and why is it required?
faq.answer: Inference.sh is the cloud service that runs the headless browser. You need an account to use the belt CLI tool that this skill depends on.

faq.question: Can this skill bypass CAPTCHAs?
faq.answer: No, this skill cannot bypass CAPTCHAs or advanced bot detection systems. It works with standard web pages only.

faq.question: How do I handle login pages that require two-factor authentication?
faq.answer: Use the session persistence feature to complete the 2FA step manually, then let the AI agent continue with the authenticated session.

faq.question: Why are my element references not working?
faq.answer: Element references become stale after page navigation or dynamic content changes. Always call the snapshot function to get fresh references.

faq.question: Can I run multiple browser sessions at once?
faq.answer: Yes, you can create multiple parallel sessions by using different session IDs. Each session maintains its own browser context.

faq.question: How do I save screenshots or videos?
faq.answer: Screenshots are returned as base64 data or files. Enable record_video during open to capture video, which is returned when you close the session.
